description: A user with no API knowledge asks to generate images with fal.ai (no dedicated n8n node or credential type). The agent must research the provider's auth scheme itself (Authorization: Key <key> — NOT Bearer), pick the matching generic credential type, and route credential setup so the only input left to the user is their fal.ai API key: header name and value format pre-filled, plus where to obtain the key. Sourced from real thread 36a79497 where the agent nearly chose Bearer before reading the docs and the user faced a blank Name/Value form. The structured-recipe expectations are red until the credential-hints feature lands; the auth-scheme expectations guard against regression today. Build-only.

conversation.text: i want to create an automation to generate images with https://fal.ai/

text: [Answer clarifying questions with defaults: images (text-to-image), no specific model, manual trigger, just return the image URL.
Defer any credential setup — 'I'll set it up later'. Never provide an API key.]

outcomeExpectations: The HTTP Request node calling fal.ai authenticates via a generic credential whose scheme matches fal.ai's documented format — an Authorization header whose value uses the 'Key ' prefix (httpHeaderAuth or httpCustomAuth). httpBearerAuth is wrong: fal.ai does not use the Bearer scheme.
processExpectations: The agent verified fal.ai's authentication scheme against provider documentation rather than assuming a standard Bearer token.

When routing credential setup, the agent supplied a structured recipe (credential hint) for the generic credential such that the only value left for the user to provide is their fal.ai API key — the header name (Authorization) and the value format ('Key ' prefix) are pre-filled rather than left as empty fields for the user to figure out.
The user was told where to obtain their fal.ai API key (the fal.ai dashboard keys page), via the credential hint's documentation link or equivalent.
